description: Converge the completed Phase 7 foundational content seed from origin/phase-7-foundational-content-seed onto main by reapplying README entries for Theories, Coordination Patterns, Frameworks, and Related Lists, updating docs/taxonomy.md to reflect on-main seeding status, preserving Scope/Contributing/Community sections, and passing make check, make test, and git diff --check.

context.customerAsk: Converge the completed Phase 7 foundational content seed onto main. Reapply the intended README additions for Theories, Coordination Patterns, Frameworks, and Related Lists from the completed origin/phase-7-foundational-content-seed work without weakening existing scope, contribution, or community sections. Keep entries canonical, factual, non-promotional, description-final-period compliant, and alphabetized within each section. Update docs/taxonomy.md so its Phase 7 status note reflects that foundational seeding is now present on main for those four sections. Keep the change narrow: do not start new categories, do not add extra docs churn, and keep make check, make test, and git diff --check passing.
context.problem: Phase 7 foundational content was completed on branch phase-7-foundational-content-seed but is not on main. The default branch still has empty Theories, Coordination Patterns, Frameworks, and Related Lists sections, and docs/taxonomy.md still states Phase 7 content seeding has not started. Contributors lack on-main exemplars for entry format, tone, category placement, and alphabetization.
context.solution: Integrate the seed-branch README entries and taxonomy status update onto main as a focused two-file repair. Reapply eighteen curated entries validated on origin/phase-7-foundational-content-seed, update docs/taxonomy.md to state foundational seeding is present on main for the four seeded sections, preserve Scope/Contributing/Community prose, leave deferred sections empty, and verify make check, make test, and git diff --check pass.

acceptanceCriteria: README Theories, Coordination Patterns, Frameworks, and Related Lists each contain the intended seed-branch entries (18 total) using - [Resource Name](URL) - Description. format
Every description ends with a period, uses factual non-promotional tone, and entries are alphabetized by link text within each section with no duplicate URLs in README.md
README Scope, Contributing, and Community sections remain present and are not weakened, shortened, or contradicted
Deferred README sections (Protocols and Interfaces, Benchmarks, Research Papers, Blog Posts, Case Studies, Examples and Templates) receive no new entries; no new categories or unrelated doc churn
docs/taxonomy.md Phase 7 status prose states foundational seeding is present on main for Theories, Coordination Patterns, Frameworks, and Related Lists
Changed files are limited to README.md, docs/taxonomy.md Phase 7 status update, and planning artifacts
Quality gate: make check, make test, and git diff --check all pass from the repository root

userStories.title: Integrate Theories entries onto main

acceptanceCriteria: README Theories contains five entries matching the seed branch: Actor Model, An Introduction to MultiAgent Systems, Blackboard Architecture, Contract Net Protocol, and Swarm Intelligence
Entries appear below the section intro in alphabetical order by link text with exact resource names and descriptions ending in a period
make check passes after Theories integration
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Update taxonomy Phase 7 status for main convergence
userStories.description: As a maintainer or factory operator tracking phase status, I want docs/taxonomy.md to reflect that Phase 7 foundational seeding is present on main so documentation matches repository state.
acceptanceCriteria: docs/taxonomy.md Phase 7 content-seeding prose states foundational seeding is present on main for Theories, Coordination Patterns, Frameworks, and Related Lists
Taxonomy notes that Blog Posts, Case Studies, Benchmarks, Research Papers, Protocols and Interfaces, and Examples and Templates remain empty for later batches
Category definitions, include/exclude rules, and README section headings in taxonomy are unchanged
No new README entries are added in deferred sections as part of this story
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Verify integration repair quality gates and section integrity
userStories.description: As a maintainer merging the integration repair, I want end-to-end verification that seeded content landed correctly and repository gates pass without regressing governance sections.
acceptanceCriteria: From repository root, make check exits 0
From repository root, make test exits 0
git diff --check reports no whitespace errors on changed files
README Scope, Contributing, and Community sections remain present and unweakened with no contradictory edits
Deferred README sections contain no new resource entries
Changed files are limited to README.md, docs/taxonomy.md, and planning artifacts with no unrelated cleanup
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
